these viral smores bars are so easy and so yummy 🍫😋 Ingredients: 1 bag semisweet chocolate chips 2 cups mini marshmallows 1 sleeve graham crackers Instructions: In a large mixing bowl, break graham crackers into small pieces. Top with mini marshmallows. In a small microwave-safe bowl, microwave chocolate chips for 30-second increments, stirring each time until melted with a glossy consistency. Allow to cool slightly and pour over graham crackers and marshmallows. Mix well. Transfer mixture to parchment-lined square baking pan and allow to set in freezer for 30-60 minutes until firm. 🎬 TREND — o café que revela a mensagem A câmera está posicionada como se fôssemos o copo transparente colocado sob a máquina de espresso. À medida que o café vai enchendo, o líquido cobre o fundo do copo e o texto começa a aparecer — revelando informações como “Estamos abertos”, horário de funcionamento, dias da semana e a marca do local. 💡 IDEIA — usar o próprio preparo como revelação O enchimento do copo funciona como efeito de transição, revelando a mensagem aos poucos enquanto o café é servido. 🔥 USOS POSSÍVEIS • Informar horário de funcionamento de forma criativa • Revelar promoções ou mensagens curtas • Inserir marca ou assinatura visual do negócio 📌 Crédito da ideia: @gleeful_coffee #PadocaCriativa #TrendDoDia #Viral #cafeteria #conteudocriativo #marketingvisual

Bakery style chocolate chip cookies 🍪  Ingredients  3/4 cup (170g) unsalted butter, cold and cubed  3/4 cup (150g) dark brown sugar 1/3 cup (65g) granulated sugar 1 large egg + 1 egg yolk, room temp 2 tsp vanilla bean paste 2 cups (240g) all-purpose flour 1 tsp cornstarch 1 tsp espresso powder 1/2 tsp baking soda 1 tbsp milk powder  1/4 tsp baking powder 3/4 tsp salt 1 cup semi sweet chocolate chips 3/4 milk chocolate chips  Flaky sea salt for topping Method : In a bowl add all purpose flour, baking soda, baking powder, milk powder, espresso powder , corn starch and salt. Mix well to combine. In a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, add the cold cubed butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar. Mix on medium speed for about 2–3 minutes, until the mixture looks creamy.  Add the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla bean paste. Mix until fully combined, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture. Mix on low speed just until combined—do not overmix. Stir in the semi-sweet and milk chocolate chips until evenly distributed.  Cover and refrigerate the dough for at least 2 hours ( or up to 24 hours for deeper flavor and thicker cookies). Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Scoop large portions of dough (about ounce each) and roll loosely into balls. For a bakery look, keep them tall rather than flattened. Bake for 15-16 minutes, or until Edges are set and lightly golden. Centers are still soft and slightly underbaked. Sprinkle flaky sea salt on top immediately after baking. Let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5–10 minutes before transferring to a wire rack. #cookies #cookierecipes #chocolatechip #cookierecipe

“Dog lovers” vs dog lovers when it comes to greeting dogs! ETA: “dog lovers” could also be replaced with “uniformed, uneducated, and/or inconsiderate dog lovers” vs “informed, educated, and considerate dog lovers” if that makes you more comfortable 🫶 One of my biggest pet peeves is people baby talking to my dog and pretending like I’m interrupting? Bizarre! And in case you didn’t know because it’s something that’s been so normalized, putting your hands in dogs’ faces is unsafe. Dogs don’t need to sniff you up close like that and it’s putting unneeded pressure on them (often leading to bites). The best way to greet a dog is calm, neutral, and honestly just ignoring them. If you want to say hi to a dog, ask the owner first. Try to make yourself small by turning to the side and crouching. Allow the dog to approach you first. Some dogs are super friendly and love people! This doesn’t mean it’s ok to pet or approach without asking their handler! The safest way to greet any dog is by admiring from afar. Admittedly, if I know someone’s dog well, I might be a bit of the former “dog lover” and be excitedly greeting them 😂 It’s different when you know the dog! This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss
